title: Unclear build pull image policy in documentation
body:
### Issue Description

Podman build documentation lead to some confusion at work the other day.

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/1443436/9a61617b-06f0-4c03-8a15-e6901e1d2117)

[A search shows](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Acontainers%2Fpodman+%22Pull+image+policy.+The+default+is%22&type=code) the default is defined in both [pull.image.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e38c713f3f23ebd98b7d798aa32ee92e767c4a9f/docs/source/markdown/options/pull.image.md?plain=1#L7) and where pull is included [podman-build.1.md.in](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e38c713f3f23ebd98b7d798aa32ee92e767c4a9f/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md.in#L316).

Looks like only a doc issue, the [default is `missing`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e38c713f3f23ebd98b7d798aa32ee92e767c4a9f/cmd/podman/common/build.go#L74).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Navigate to https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-build.1.html#pull-policy
2. Verify two defaults are showing for pull image policy


### Describe the results you received

Confusion over build pull image policy default.

### Describe the results you expected

A clear default for build pull image policy.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

This is in the man page on website: https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-build.1.html#pull-policy.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Earlier versions only said the default pull policy was `always`, and did not have the line about `missing`. I believe what they are trying to say is "if you do not specify `--pull`, the default value is `missing`, and if you specify `--pull` without a value, the default value is `always`". But it is not how it comes across.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think(?) this may be a regression introduced by commit https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/4a4d86d40f9137144103147e7a0ba74fbf3aaca8, specifically this line:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/850db76dd78a0641eddb9ee19ee6f60d2c59bcfa/cmd/podman/common/build.go#L312

That is, the `--pull` flag no longer behaves the same as `--pull=true`,  contrary to the behavior described by @vrothberg in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20124#issuecomment-1734930621.

> [...] The default behavior is "missing" but `--pull` (without a string value) is the same as `--pull=true`.  [...]

Standalone test case:
```console
$ podman -v
podman version 5.5.1
$ echo "FROM alpine:3.22" > Dockerfile
$ podman build --pull --log-level=debug .
...
DEBU[0000] FROM "alpine:3.22"                           
STEP 1/1: FROM alpine:3.22
DEBU[0000] Pulling image alpine:3.22 (policy: missing)  
...
$ podman build --pull=true --log-level=debug .
...
DEBU[0000] FROM "alpine:3.22"                           
STEP 1/1: FROM alpine:3.22
DEBU[0000] Pulling image alpine:3.22 (policy: always)   
...
```

Note that this was previously fixed via PR #8210, but no test was added to cover it.

> [...] If the `--pull` command IS specified or specified with an argument of `true`, the build will always pull the image from the registries.  This uses the pullOption `PullAlways`. [...]

It seems the above mentioned issue was resolved in Podman v5.6.0.
```console
$ podman -v
podman version 5.6.0
$ echo "FROM alpine:3.22" > Dockerfile
$ podman build --pull --log-level=debug . 2>&1 | grep "policy:"
time="2025-09-10T10:10:00+02:00" level=debug msg="Pulling image alpine:3.22 (policy: always)"
$ podman build --pull=true --log-level=debug . 2>&1 | grep "policy:"
time="2025-09-10T10:10:04+02:00" level=debug msg="Pulling image alpine:3.22 (policy: always)"
```

The documentation issue was also resolved in commit 64091777fe78fac407f472cf4eb2d3e66719f23a, so I think this can be closed.
